title: Acianthera sulcata
taxonomy: Acianthera sulcata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This orchid species belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and is further classified into the tribe Epidendreae. Acianthera sulcata is a genus of orchids that contains over 600 species, and within this genus, it is part of the section Pseudopodacanthella. The classification of Acianthera sulcata is constantly evolving as new research and discoveries are made. Further classification may involve subgenus or subfamily levels, but the current understanding places Acianthera sulcata within the family Orchidaceae.

intro: Acianthera sulcata, also known as the Sulcate Acianthera, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce the risk of infections, and alleviate respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Acianthera sulcata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
